methodology: Heuristic engagement classification based on watch context signals
source: User interview (Apr 3, 2026) — user confirmed some videos were watched intently while others autoplayed

engagement_model.active: Intentionally watched — sought out, searched for, or deliberately clicked. Represents focused learning.
engagement_model.passive: Autoplayed or background — part of a viewing session but not actively sought. Learning by osmosis.

strong_active_signals: Video title matches a search query in the same session
Video followed by a related commit within 24 hours (correlation strength: strong/very_strong)
Channel is one of the user's top-5 subscribed/frequented
Multiple videos from same creator on same day (binge watching = active)
Videos watched during the user's active hours (9 PM - 1 AM) are more likely active
strong_passive_signals: Videos watched in rapid succession (>5/hour with no search queries)
Generic news/roundup channels (AI Daily Brief, etc.)
Videos outside the user's core topics (voice_audio, video_gen when no related commits)
Videos during low-commit days (no commits that day or next day)
uncertain_signals: Topic partially overlaps with commit activity

coding_agents.rationale: Coding agent videos directly relevant to daily work. Searched-for content. High correlation with commits.

agent_arch.rationale: Agent architecture is core to Liminal's RalphLoop design. User actively researched this.

ml_fundamentals.rationale: Mixed — some papers actively studied (RL, ES), some background lectures autoplayed.

llm_models.rationale: Large volume (481 videos). Many are model release news, autoplayed in sessions. Searched models were active.

local_ai.rationale: Running models locally was actively pursued for Liminal's model tiers.

creative_ai.rationale: Peripherally relevant to Liminal's aesthetic module. More exploratory/passive.

philosophy_ethics.rationale: Background interest, not directly tied to commits.

video_gen.rationale: Sora/Runway curiosity, not actively used in Liminal until later.

voice_audio.rationale: Directly relevant to Liminal's audio module development.

other_ai.rationale: Catch-all category — mostly autoplayed news and roundup content.

creator_engagement_dimension.methodology: Creator loyalty overrides topic classification. Tier 1 creators are actively followed regardless of what they cover — user confirmed. Tier 2 creators are actively watched when topic is relevant.

tier_1_creators.name: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
tier_1_creators.rationale: 150 videos over 7 months, consistent weekly watch. User confirmed as KEY creator — watched regardless of topic. Top creator by volume.

tier_1_creators.name: IndyDevDan
tier_1_creators.rationale: Developer-focused AI content directly relevant to Claude Code workflow. Peak Feb-Mar 2026. User confirmed as KEY creator — same caliber as Nate B Jones, above Berman in importance.

tier_1_creators.name: Jake Van Clief
tier_1_creators.rationale: All 9 videos in Mar-Apr 2026. Agent workflow and Claude Code customization content. Titles like 'Stop Building AI Agents' directly influenced Era 9-10. User confirmed as KEY creator — quality over quantity, same caliber as Nate B Jones.

tier_2_creators.name: Matthew Berman
tier_2_creators.rationale: 85 videos over 2.5 years. Consistent watching. Active when covering tools/models. Less directly influential than Tier 1 despite higher volume.

tier_2_creators.name: WorldofAI
tier_2_creators.rationale: Open-source AI tool reviews directly relevant to Liminal's stack decisions.

tier_2_creators.name: AI Warehouse
tier_2_creators.rationale: Agent learning videos (RL/ES) directly correlated with RalphLoop implementation.

tier_2_creators.name: Nate Herk | AI Automation
tier_2_creators.rationale: AI automation workflows directly relevant to multi-agent coordination work.

creator_engagement_dimension.note: Creator engagement is independent of topic. A Nate B Jones video about video generation gets active engagement even though the 'video_gen' topic category is only 20% active. Tier 1 creators (Nate B Jones, IndyDevDan, Jake Van Clief) are a stronger signal than topic relevance. Jake Van Clief's 9 videos had outsized influence — discovered late but directly shaped Era 9-10 decisions. User validated Apr 3, 2026.

videos: Claude Code token hacks, Claude limit burning
Temperature in neural nets, learning vs exploration

likely_passive_examples.pattern: AI Daily Brief / news roundup channels
likely_passive_examples.rationale: These are short-form news videos typically autoplayed in morning/evening sessions

likely_passive_examples.pattern: 5+ videos from different channels in same hour
likely_passive_examples.rationale: Rapid succession suggests autoplay, not deliberate seeking

likely_passive_examples.pattern: Non-core topic videos on zero-commit days

estimated_overall_split.note: Passive viewing is still meaningful — 'learning by osmosis' builds intuition even without focused attention. The user internalized concepts from autoplayed videos that later surfaced in commit messages and architectural decisions.

disclaimer: These are heuristic estimates, not measured data. YouTube Takeout does not include watch duration or completion rates. User validation needed for specific videos or date ranges.
